name: 2024 Iranian Parliamentary Election (12th Majlis)
description: The March 2024 parliamentary election produced Iran's lowest-ever turnout at approximately 41% nationally and below 25% in Tehran. The [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) approved only around 15,200 of 48,847 registered candidates to run. Principlists won 233 of 290 seats. In Tehran, ultraconservatives from the [Front of Islamic Revolution Stability](/orgs/front-of-islamic-revolution-stability) outperformed the establishment SHANA coalition backed by Speaker [Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf](/people/mohammad-bagher-ghalibaf). [Mahmoud Nabavian](/people/mahmoud-nabavian) topped the Tehran ballot, followed by [Amir-Hossein Sabeti](/people/amir-hossein-sabeti) and [Hamid Rasaee](/people/hamid-rasaee). [Masoud Pezeshkian](/people/masoud-pezeshkian) won a Tabriz seat but resigned it in July 2024 upon election as President. A second round was held on 10 May 2024.
editorial_notes: Record-low turnout. Paydari outperformed establishment SHANA coalition in Tehran. Pezeshkian resigned seat July 2024 after winning presidency.
